title: 10 best herbal essential oils for post nasal drip
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for post nasal drip are a natural and effective way to alleviate the discomfort and congestion caused by excess mucus in the back of the throat. These oils, derived from plants such as peppermint, eucalyptus, and ginger, help to thin and clear out mucus, reducing coughing and congestion. For example, peppermint oil's cooling properties can ease a stuffy nose and promote easier breathing, while eucalyptus oil's decongestant properties can help relieve sinus pressure and promote drainage. By using these oils in a diffuser or applying them topically, individuals can find relief from post nasal drip and improve their overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with post nasal drip because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses. The oil's expectorant properties also work to loosen and clear out excess mucus, providing relief from congestion and sinus pressure. Additionally, Tasmanian blue gum's antiviral and antibacterial properties help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to post nasal drip, promoting a healthy and balanced respiratory system.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and Antiviral Properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil has been shown to exhibit potent antibacterial and antiviral activity, which can help combat underlying infections and reduce the severity of post-nasal drip symptoms.

Expectorant and Decongestant Properties: The oil's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, while its decongestant properties reduce swelling in the nasal tissues, providing relief from congestion and discomfort associated with post-nasal drip.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the nasal passages and sinuses, which can exacerbate post-nasal drip symptoms and other respiratory issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds that help soothe and calm inflammation in the nasal passages, providing relief from post nasal drip.
Barks: The bark contains a high concentration of eucalyptol, which is known for its decongestant properties and helps to clear mucus and reduce congestion associated with post nasal drip.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in abundance carefully avoiding any signs of decay or mold.
Dry the harvested eucalyptus globulus leaves by spreading them out in a single layer on paper towels for 7 days.
Steam distill the dried eucalyptus globulus leaves using a still apparatus with 10 grams of leaves and 500 milliliters of water for 4 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil and filter it through cheesecloth into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids immediately after distillation.
Store the filtered eucalyptus globulus essential oil in a cool dark place at room temperature for up to 6 months or in the refrigerator for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with post nasal drip because its menthol content creates a thin layer of oil that coats the nasal passages, helping to reduce congestion and calm irritated membranes. The oil's decongestant properties also help to shrink swollen blood vessels in the nose, further reducing excess mucus production and runny nose symptoms. Additionally, peppermint oil's natural cooling sensation can help to soothe an itchy or scratchy throat, providing relief from post nasal drip discomfort.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Peppermint oil helps to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, making it easier to expel, which can provide relief from postnasal drip.
Decongestant properties: Peppermint oil's ability to reduce swelling in the nasal tissues can help to relieve congestion and sinus pressure associated with postnasal drip.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint oil's anti-inflammatory compounds can help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the throat and nasal passages, which can contribute to the sensation of postnasal drip.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant are used to make essential oil due to their ability to relieve congestion and ease respiratory issues associated with post nasal drip.
Leaves: Peppermint leaves are used to create essential oil, which helps to thin mucus and clear out nasal passages, providing relief from post nasal drip symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from the garden or store-bought, ensuring approximately 2 cups of leaves are collected for distillation.
Combine the harvested peppermint leaves with 4 cups of distilled water in a large glass still and secure the lid tightly.
Heat the mixture over medium heat until it reaches a temperature of 212°f, allowing steam to rise from the surface and condense into oil.
Allow the distillation process to continue for at least 30 minutes, ensuring the peppermint oil content is maximized with an expected yield of 1-2%.
Transfer the resulting essential oil to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, store in a cool place, and use within six months to ensure optimal potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with post nasal drip because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses. The oil's decongestant properties also work to thin out mucus, making it easier for your body to expel, thereby providing relief from congestion and drips. Additionally, rosemary essential oils have antimicrobial properties that help to combat infections that may be contributing to postnasal drip, promoting a healthy respiratory system and alleviating symptoms associated with this condition.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Rosemary essential oil's antimicrobial property helps combat bacterial and fungal infections in the nasal passages, reducing inflammation and congestion associated with postnasal drip.
Expectorant property: Rosemary essential oil's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages and sinuses, relieving congestion and postnasal drip symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory property: Rosemary essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages, sinuses, and throat, providing relief from postnasal drip discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, rosemary roots help to reduce inflammation and congestion associated with post nasal drip.
Leaves: Packed with camphor, bornyl acetate, and other bioactive compounds, rosemary leaves have natural decongestant properties that ease sinus pressure and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosemary leaves from a healthy plant for a minimum of one ounce dried or four ounces fresh material.
Dry the harvested rosemary leaves by air drying them for two to three weeks or using a food dehydrator on low heat.
Steam distill the dried rosemary leaves using a still with a condenser at a temperature range of 160 to 180 degrees fahrenheit for six to eight hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water by decanting it into a dark glass bottle and storing it in a cool dry place for up to two years.
Perform quality control checks on the essential oil by testing its color clarity and aroma before using it topically or in aromatherapy blends at a dosage of 10 to 15 drops per application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with post nasal drip because of its natural antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and decongestant properties. When inhaled or applied topically, thyme oil can help soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the nose and throat, reducing excess mucus production and promoting drainage. This can provide relief from congestion and coughing associated with post nasal drip, allowing for easier breathing and improved overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial Properties: Thyme essential oil has potent antibacterial properties that can help combat bacterial infections that may be contributing to postnasal drip, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Expectorant Properties: Thyme essential oil acts as a natural expectorant, loosening and breaking down mucus, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion associated with postnasal drip.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: Thyme essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages and sinuses, providing relief from the discomfort and congestion caused by postnasal drip.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are rich in antioxidants and antibacterial properties that help alleviate congestion and reduce inflammation associated with post nasal drip.
Leaves: Thyme leaves contain expectorant compounds that help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, providing relief from coughs and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thyme leaves from the garden or storehouse to ensure optimal potency and flavor.
Dry the harvested thyme leaves in a warm oven at 150°f for 2 hours to prevent bacterial growth.
Steam distill the dried thyme leaves in a clevenger apparatus with 50 grams of material per liter of distilled water for 4 hours.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil using a coffee filter or paper towel to remove impurities.
Transfer the filtered essential oil into dark glass bottles and store them in a cool, dry place for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen essential oils helps with post nasal drip because of its natural expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages. The menthol content in wintergreen oil also provides a soothing and cooling sensation that can help to reduce congestion and discomfort associated with post nasal drip. Additionally, wintergreen's decongestant properties can help to shrink swollen nasal tissues and open up airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the feeling of mucus dripping down the back of the throat.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Wintergreen essential oil's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages, which can alleviate symptoms of postnasal drip such as congestion and discomfort.

Decongestant property: The decongestant properties of wintergreen essential oil help to shrink swollen mucous membranes and reduce mucus production, providing relief from stuffy noses and sinuses associated with postnasal drip.

Antimicrobial property: Wintergreen essential oil's antimicrobial properties help to combat infections that can cause postnasal drip, such as the common cold or sinusitis, by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and viruses.
remedy_parts: Roots: Wintergreen roots are rich in methyl salicylate, a compound that helps relieve congestion and reduce inflammation associated with post nasal drip.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of the wintergreen plant contains a high concentration of wintergreen oil, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate post nasal drip symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems in the morning for optimal oil quality.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 4-6 hours.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a small steam distillation apparatus at 100°c for 1 hour.
Collect the essential oil produced during distillation and store it in dark glass bottles with tight lids at room temperature.
Use the freshly distilled essential oil within 24 hours of production or blend with carrier oils to extend shelf life for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree essential oils helps with post nasal drip because of its natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as eucalyptol and terpinen-4-ol, work to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, allowing mucus to drain more effectively and alleviating congestion. Additionally, tea tree oil's antimicrobial properties help combat underlying infections that can contribute to post nasal drip, providing relief from persistent coughing and sinus pressure.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Tea Tree Essential Oil helps combat post-nasal drip by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause respiratory infections, reducing inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal mucous membranes, allowing for easier breathing and relieving sinus pressure associated with post-nasal drip.

Decongestant properties: Tea Tree Essential Oil contains compounds that help constrict blood vessels and reduce nasal congestion, making it easier to breathe and relieving the sensation of mucus running down the back of the throat.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the tea tree contain high amounts of cineole, a compound known for its decongestant and expectorant properties, making it effective in relieving symptoms of post nasal drip.
Seeds: Tea tree seeds contain a significant amount of terpinen-4-ol, an antibacterial compound that helps to combat infections causing post nasal drip.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of melaleuca alternifolia, ensuring they are free from contamination and moisture.
Clean the harvested leaves by gently rinsing them in distilled water and patting dry with a paper towel.
Dry the cleaned leaves in a well-ventilated area at room temperature for 7 to 10 days or until they reach 10% moisture content.
Steam distill the dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves using a ratio of 1 part herbs to 2 parts water for 90 minutes to produce essential oil.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities and preserve its quality.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with post nasal drip because of its natural anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. The oil's active compounds, such as camphor and cineole, work together to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, allowing for easier drainage and relief from congestion. Additionally, sage oil's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out excess mucus, providing temporary and effective relief from postnasal drip.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sage essential oil helps reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, which can alleviate congestion and relieve postnasal drip symptoms.
Expectorant: The essential oil's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal cavity, reducing the feeling of a constant runny nose and postnasal drip.

Antihistamine-like properties: Sage essential oil has been shown to have antihistamine-like effects, which can help reduce the production of excess mucus in response to allergens or irritants, thereby minimizing postnasal drip.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and clear congestion in the sinuses, making them effective for treating post nasal drip.
Buds: Sage buds are rich in flavonoids and terpenes, which work together to reduce inflammation and open up airways, providing relief from post nasal drip.
remedy_recipe: Harvest salvia officinalis leaves and flowers in large quantities for distillation.
Combine 250 grams of freshly harvested salvia officinalis with 500 milliliters of still water in the distillation apparatus.
Distill the mixture using steam at atmospheric pressure for 3 hours to produce essential oil and hydrosol.
Strain the resulting distillate through a coffee filter into dark glass bottles for storage.
Allow the essential oil to mature in a cool dark place for 2 weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curry plant essential oils helps with post nasal drip because of its natural anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. The oil's active compounds, such as citral and geraniol, work to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, allowing mucus to drain more effectively. This can help alleviate symptoms of postnasal drip, including congestion, sinus pressure, and excessive mucus production. Additionally, curry plant essential oils' natural expectorant properties help loosen and clear out mucus, providing relief from discomfort and promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Curry Plant essential oil's antimicrobial properties help to combat bacterial, viral, and fungal infections in the nasal passages, reducing inflammation and congestion associated with post-nasal drip.

Expectorant properties: The expectorant properties of Curry Plant essential oil help to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier for the body to expel excess mucus and relieving symptoms of post-nasal drip.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Curry Plant essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating discomfort and congestion associated with post-nasal drip.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the curry plant contain volatile compounds that help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, making them effective in alleviating symptoms of post nasal drip.
Barks: The barks of the curry plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, providing relief from post nasal drip.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh helichrysum italicum leaves and flowers in the early morning to ensure optimal potency.
Cleanse the harvested plant material by gently rinsing it with distilled water to remove impurities.
Steam distill the plant material using a clevenger apparatus for 4 hours to extract the essential oils.
Combine the extracted essential oil with carrier oil such as sweet almond or grapeseed oil at a ratio of 5% helichrysum italicum to 95%.
Store the final product in dark glass bottles and keep it refrigerated for up to 6 months to preserve shelf life.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Purple coneflower essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, relieving postnasal drip symptoms.
Antiviral and antimicrobial property: The oil's antiviral and antimicrobial properties can help combat underlying infections causing postnasal drip by inhibiting the growth of viruses and bacteria.

Expectorant property: Purple coneflower essential oil's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, making it easier for your body to expel excess phlegm and reducing congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and relieve congestion caused by post nasal drip.
Buds: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, buds can help reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections that contribute to post nasal drip.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea flowers in the morning after dew has dried off at dawn.
Dry the harvested flowers by spreading them out in a single layer on paper towels for two weeks.
Steam distill 100 grams of dried echinacea purpurea flowers with 500 milliliters of distilled water for four hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distillation mixture using a separatory funnel and collect it in a dark glass bottle.
Store the extracted essential oil in an amber-colored glass vial, away from direct sunlight, at room temperature for up to six months.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower essential oils helps with post nasal drip because its anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the nasal passages, reducing congestion and excess mucus production. The oil's natural antimicrobial properties also help combat underlying infections that can cause postnasal drip. Additionally, purple coneflower's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus, providing fast and effective relief from annoying postnasal drip symptoms.

English lavender essential oils helps with post nasal drip because its calming and soothing properties can help reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages. The oil's antiviral and antibacterial properties also work to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the condition. Additionally, the decongestant properties of lavender oil can help thin out mucus, making it easier to expel and alleviating symptoms of post nasal drip.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine property: English Lavender essential oil's antihistamine property helps to reduce histamine levels in the body, which can alleviate symptoms such as nasal congestion and postnasal drip caused by allergies or colds.

Expectorant property: The expectorant properties of English Lavender essential oil help to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel and providing relief from postnasal drip and coughing.

Anti-inflammatory property: English Lavender essential oil's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, which can contribute to postnasal drip and sinus pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which help to soothe and calm congestion in the nasal passages.
Flowers: Containing linalool and linalyl acetate, these fragrant blooms promote relaxation and ease sinus pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at dawn, avoiding any that are wilted or damaged.
Steam distill the harvested flowers using a copper still and 1000ml of distilled water for 90 minutes.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the distillation vapors in the condenser coil, resulting in approximately 1ml.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter or cotton cloth to remove any impurities and sedimentation, taking about 10 minutes.
Bottle the filtered lavandula angustifolia essential oil in a dark glass vial with a tight-fitting cap, storing it in a cool, dry place for future use.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat post nasal drip with herbal essential oils is by blending eucalyptus oil, peppermint oil, and thyme oil with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Apply this blend topically on the chest, neck, and behind the ears to ease congestion and open airways. Inhale the vapors directly from the bottle or use a diffuser for added relief. This natural approach helps reduce post nasal drip symptoms without harsh side effects.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to post nasal drip that are treated with herbal essential oils are sinusitis, bronchitis, and allergies. Oils like eucalyptus, peppermint, and lavender help relieve congestion and inflammation in the respiratory tract. Eucalyptus oil, in particular, is known for its decongestant properties and is often used to treat sinus infections and colds. These herbal essential oils can be inhaled directly or added to steam baths to provide relief from these conditions.
